_comment: Kiro arrangement (targets Kiro 0.9+: Agent Skills, custom subagents, auto-steering, hooks). The generator emits, per role: an Agent Skill skills/<id>/SKILL.md (frontmatter + body, agentskills.io-compatible) bundling its shared/ templates and scripts/, plus (when subagent=true) a thin agents/<id>.md that points at the skill. It also generates ONE lean auto-steering orchestrator (steering/devloop.md) that only sequences the phases, and the hooks. Authored separately (not generated): README.md. The skill bodies are the single source — subagents and steering never restate them.

steering.name: devloop
steering.description: DevLoop business-analysis chain — turning a feature idea into requirements, INVEST user stories, a story review, and a Jira organization plan, grounded in compiled LLM-Wikis. Use when the user asks to gather requirements, write stories/tickets, or plan a backlog.

hooks.name: DevLoop: lint wikis
hooks.description: Validate every DevLoop wiki for broken [[wikilinks]] and orphans, including cross-wiki [[namespace:Concept]] links. Run manually from the Agent Hooks panel.

hooks.name: DevLoop: sync wikis
hooks.description: Clone/pull every git-backed wiki and report which sources changed since the last build (exits non-zero on a git failure). Run manually from the Agent Hooks panel.

mcp._note: Example only — installed to .kiro/settings/mcp.json ONLY IF that file is absent (never clobbers your real MCP config). The Context Librarian can read SharePoint via a Microsoft Graph / SharePoint MCP server; there is no single official one, so fill in `args` with the server you use, set `disabled` to false, and provide the token via the referenced env var (never hardcode secrets).
